_meta.purpose: Single source of truth for OOS-gate criteria. All validation scripts read these constants instead of hardcoding. Bumping version forces a re-validation of all deployed configs.
_meta.reasoning: Yesterday's TIA case: deployed at OOS n=10 / PF 3.49 (just-passed old gate). 24h later: OOS n=9 / PF 0.65. Single day of data drift broke it. Tightening every threshold by ~50% to absorb that drift before it reaches deployment.

_comments.min_oos_n_trades: was 10 in old gate — bumped to 15 to give buffer for ~3% daily window drift
_comments.min_oos_pf: was 1.2 — bumped to 1.5 because PFs near 1.2 swing into <1.0 with one bad week
_comments.max_neg_quartiles: was 1 — tightened to 0 because any negative quartile signals regime fragility
_comments.min_pf_above_random: was 0.3 — bumped to 0.6 because sub-0.3 differentials are noise
_comments.min_sensitivity_pf: was 1.0 — bumped to 1.2 to ensure params aren't on a knife-edge
